sourceMovie.title: Dredd
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Audiences latched onto Dredd (2012) as a action theatrical experience: big enough for a group watch, specific enough to reward a second viewing once you know where it's headed. The action and science fiction blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Men in Black
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Dredd clicked for you, Men in Black (1997) is a strong next stop: both trade in action and science fiction, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Looper
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Dredd clicked for you, Looper (2012) is a strong next stop: both trade in action and science fiction, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Dredd?
faq.answer: Try Men in Black, Looper, The Matrix Revolutions—each shares DNA with Dredd while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Dredd?
faq.answer: Dredd (2012) is best described as Action, Science Fiction. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Dredd worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.9/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this action lane.
